mentions.note: Cramer wants to buy Netflix. Warner Bros. studio contemplation was prudent optionality review; down 13% is good entry; will outperform.
Caller Frank: stock at $83 down 11% after movie studio bidding war ended; Cramer to investigate disconnect; possible undervaluation.
FANG stock from Feb 2013; obvious streaming leader; long-term secular growth with consistent earnings.
High-quality growth name in FANG bundle; example of recession-resilient stock; subject to ETF-driven volatility despite business independence from peers.
Leads by example on conference calls with clear narrative; balances analyst detail with retail audience needs; model for executive communication.
Juggernaut company; can build great studio independently; came from nowhere to create greatest entertainment company on earth; benefit of doubt warranted.
$2.8B breakup check from failed Warner deal; $20B content investment + ad revenue doubling; leg into weakness, not all at once.
Walking away from Warner Bros. bid is capital-disciplined move; relieved balance sheet pressure. Stock moved 10% but Cramer favors buyer entry here.
Down since stock split; Cramer bullish on Ted Sarandos and Warner Bros. Discovery deal as transformational content consolidation for Netflix studio capabilities.
Conference call this week was show of force; completely on its game. Should be buyer of Netflix.
Pursuing Warner Bros Discovery acquisition; competing with Paramount SkyDance on deal pricing; earnings after close Tuesday.
